title: Shell Layout
description: SpecD Studio is a spec-work IDE: sidebars for changes and workspaces, a tabbed editor area, inspector for artifacts, and a bottom panel (Output, Problems, Logs). The shell also orchestrates global polling so agent activity off-screen (new changes, specs on disk) appears without keeping every change tab on a heavy refresh loop.
context: SpecD Studio is a spec-work IDE: sidebars for changes and workspaces, a tabbed editor area, inspector for artifacts, and a bottom panel (Output, Problems, Logs). The shell also orchestrates global polling so agent activity off-screen (new changes, specs on disk) appears without keeping every change tab on a heavy refresh loop.

rules.requirement: shell provides sidebar tabs inspector and bottom panel regions
rules:
The layout MUST render a unified **titlebar**, a left **primary sidebar** built on
shadcn **`Sidebar`** / **`SidebarProvider`**, a central workspace inside
**`SidebarInset`**, a right-hand inspector host, and a bottom panel host with three
tabs in order **Output**, **Problems**, **Logs**. The default selected bottom tab MUST
be **Output**.

The unified **titlebar** MUST span the full shell width (above both sidebar and main
content) on **macOS desktop** (`data-platform="darwin"`). On hosts without integrated
traffic lights (web, Windows, Linux), the titlebar MUST render only above the main
content column; the primary sidebar MUST extend to the top of the shell.

The primary sidebar MUST use shadcn **`Sidebar`** with `collapsible="icon"` and support
two states:

- **Expanded** (~16rem): **`SidebarHeader`** with **SpecD Studio** branding, a
  **`SidebarMenu`** rail (Changes / Workspaces / Graph icons with badges), then **two
  stacked blocks** — **Changes** and **Workspaces – Specs** — both visible (compact
  lists). There MUST NOT be a third Graph block in the sidebar body.
- **Collapsed** (48px icon rail): icon buttons with corner badges and tooltips.

**Graph** on the rail MUST open the center graph view only; it MUST NOT render a
sidebar body panel.

On **macOS desktop**, the fixed sidebar MUST start below the titlebar (`top` offset
matching `--studio-titlebar-height`) so native window controls do not overlap the
sidebar header. On other hosts, the sidebar MUST use full viewport height (`top: 0`).

Clicking **Changes** or **Workspaces** on the rail MUST set the center to the matching
**domain hub** and MUST NOT auto-expand the sidebar. Clicking **Graph** MUST open the
graph center view.

On **macOS desktop**, the titlebar MUST include shadcn **`SidebarTrigger`** after the
traffic-light slot. On **web / Windows / Linux**, `SidebarTrigger` MUST be embedded in
the sidebar (header top-right when expanded; first collapsed rail item with a divider
before other rail icons) and MUST NOT appear in the titlebar.

The titlebar MUST include **inline** Documentation, Notifications, and Appearance
controls on all hosts (desktop included), with safe-zone padding so controls do not
overlap native window buttons.

rules.requirement: shell provides unified titlebar for global actions
rules:
The shell MUST render a lean titlebar (~44px on desktop) containing:

- on **macOS desktop**: a non-interactive **traffic-light slot** (`--studio-safe-left`)
  followed immediately by shadcn **`SidebarTrigger`**, with the titlebar row spanning
  the full shell width above sidebar and main content
- on **web / Windows / Linux**: no `SidebarTrigger` in the titlebar; titlebar row above
  the main content column only (sidebar is full height alongside it)
- command-palette search entry (⌘K / Ctrl+K)
- **New Change** action (visible on all hosts)
- on **all** hosts including desktop: Documentation, Notifications, and Appearance as
  **inline icon buttons** (not hidden behind a default overflow menu). The search field
  MUST shrink before secondary actions are hidden.

On **web / Windows / Linux**, the shell MUST render an embedded **`SidebarTrigger`** in
the primary sidebar: top-right of the expanded header, or the first collapsed rail
icon separated by a divider from Changes / Workspaces / Graph.

The titlebar MUST NOT display **SpecD Studio** branding — that belongs in the
expanded sidebar header only.

Secondary titlebar controls MUST respect platform safe zones defined in
[`ui:design-system`](../design-system/spec.md) so they do not overlap native window
controls.

rules.requirement: sidebar collapse state is keyboard-toggleable and persisted
rules:
The shell MUST toggle sidebar expanded ↔ collapsed on **⌘B** (macOS) / **Ctrl+B**
(Windows/Linux/web).

Collapsed state (`SidebarProvider` `open`) MUST persist through `IUserStorage` and
restore on the next Studio session.

rules.requirement: workspace tree poll skips when Workspaces section is not visible
rules:
The shell MUST pass an `enabled` or equivalent visibility flag to
[`ui:hooks-workspaces-specs`](../hooks-workspaces-specs/spec.md) so workspace tree
metadata does NOT refetch on global poll when:

- the sidebar is collapsed to the icon rail **and** the center is not
  **workspaces-hub**, OR
- neither the Workspaces sidebar block nor the workspaces hub is relevant

Poll MUST run when the sidebar is expanded (stacked Workspaces block visible) OR when
`centerCtx` is **workspaces-hub** or an open **spec**.

When poll is paused, cached workspace tree data MUST remain available so re-expanding
the sidebar does not flash an empty loading state; a catch-up refetch MAY run when poll
resumes.

rules.requirement: shell provides center domain hubs for changes and workspaces
rules:
The center workspace MUST support `changes-hub` and `workspaces-hub` contexts in
addition to change, spec, graph, and empty views.

**Changes hub** MUST show change counts by lifecycle section and a list with **full
change names** (not truncated). Selecting a row MUST open that change in the center.

**Workspaces hub** MUST list workspaces and specs with **full paths** and surface spec
validation / drift status where available. Selecting a row MUST open that spec.

Compact sidebar lists MAY truncate; hubs are the readable inventory views.

Domain hub UI MUST live in dedicated modules under `packages/ui/src/shell/hubs/`
imported by `ShellLayout` — not inlined as a monolithic shell file.

rules.requirement: shell orchestrates global polling while focused
rules:
While the Studio window has focus, a **global poll** every **2–3 seconds** refreshes project scope without requiring an open change tab:

- `ui:hooks-project` → `getProject`, `getProjectStatus`
- `ui:hooks-changes-collection` + sidebars → list changes, drafts, discarded, archived, overlaps
- `ui:hooks-workspaces-specs` + workspace tree → spec tree metadata
- Graph entry → cheap `graph/status`

rules.requirement: editor area tabs support horizontal scrolling and navigation arrows
rules: When the central tab workspace or right-hand inspector host contains more tabs than the available width, the tab bar MUST support fluid horizontal scrolling (e.g. via trackpad or mouse wheel). Navigation arrows (← and →) MUST appear at the right end of the tab bar when desbordamiento exists, allowing the user to scroll programmatically. Arrows MUST be disabled visually when the scroll limit is reached in their respective direction.

rules.requirement: tab views display context via breadcrumbs
rules: To stabilize tab bar height and provide persistent context, change and spec tab views MUST display their qualified name or path (e.g. `CHANGES / <name>` or `SPECS / <path>`) in a thin breadcrumb header immediately above the horizontal tab bar. The tab bar itself MUST remain single-line and MUST NOT contain trailing name/path metadata that could cause height jumps on narrow viewports.

rules.requirement: global polling pauses when the window is unfocused
rules: The global poll interval MUST NOT run while the browser window or Electron app lacks focus.

rules.requirement: shell never imports @specd/core
rules: All data MUST flow through `SpecdDataPort` hooks; direct `@specd/core` imports are forbidden.

rules.requirement: shell applies design-system theme at application root
rules: `SpecdApp` MUST import the centralized theme from `ui:design-system` (CSS variables or equivalent) before any feature chrome renders. Shell regions (sidebar, tabs, inspector, bottom panel, status bar) MUST consume design tokens—not ad hoc colors or card-dashboard styling.

rules.requirement: shell routes archived changes through archived read port
rules: When the user opens a change from the Archive sidebar section, the shell MUST load detail via `getArchivedChange`, MUST skip active-change status polling, and MUST disable inspector save and validate. Archived artifact body reads MUST use the archived read-only artifact route, not active `getChangeArtifact`.

rules.requirement: shell routes drafted and discarded changes through read-only ports
rules: When the open change name appears in the **Drafts** or **Discarded** sidebar collection (and not archived), the shell MUST set `listSection` to `draft` or `discarded` and MUST pass it to [`ui:hooks-changes-read`](../hooks-changes-read/spec.md) (`useChangesRead`, `useChangeArtifact`, and artifact list hooks). The shell MUST NOT call `getChange` or `getChangeStatus` for that name while it is shelved or discarded. Shelved read-only MUST mirror archived UX boundaries: banner in the center column, no Save/Validate in the inspector, Monaco `readOnly`, Overview editors disabled — without altering the archived snapshot flow (`getArchivedChange`, archived banner, archived artifact list/body reads).

rules.requirement: read-only change tabs load once (no per-change polling)
rules: Global sidebar polling continues to refresh the lists, but the open drafted/discarded/archived change views are static. The shell MUST NOT schedule per-change polling (status, artifacts, tab refresh keys) for these read-only sections. Drafted changes MAY load workflow status once on open, but MUST NOT poll while kept open.

rules.requirement: validate requires drift confirmation
rules:
**Validate** and **Validate All** MUST show [`ui:validate-confirm-dialog`](../validate-confirm-dialog/spec.md) before calling `validateChange`. Scoped validate MUST pass `specId` and `artifactId` derived from the open change artifact; **Validate All** MUST validate each spec in `change.specIds`.

The **Validate All** action MUST be offered from the change **Artifacts** tab and the **Command Palette** (as "Validate change artifacts"), but only when a valid, editable change is currently selected. Change tabs that require live change APIs (Tasks, Coverage, Context) MUST show read-only messaging for archived context. Workflow and validation status from `getChangeStatus` MUST appear on the Overview tab only (not a separate Validation tab).

rules.requirement: shell delegates tab-scoped polling to visible center tabs
rules: The global poll refresh key MUST NOT force every change or spec tab to refetch. Child views MUST adopt the latest global tick only while their tab is visible (frozen refresh key while hidden) so Artifacts, Tasks, spec Overview/Dependencies, and similar tabs do not reload in the background.

rules.requirement: opening a spec auto-selects the primary artifact
rules: When the user opens a spec in the workspace tree and that spec exposes a canonical `spec.md` artifact, the shell MUST preselect that `spec.md` artifact in the right inspector automatically.

rules.requirement: bottom panel polls only the remote logs channel
rules: `readProjectLogs` polling MUST run only while **Logs** is active. **Output** and **Problems** MUST render from local session state and MUST NOT poll a dedicated studio-specific output endpoint. Switching bottom tabs MUST NOT append output lines.

rules.requirement: metadata and artifact saves log to Output
rules: Artifact save ([`ui:hooks-inspector-save`](../hooks-inspector-save/spec.md)), description/policy PATCH, successful scope dialog save, and validation MUST append to the local output buffer consumed by [`ui:bottom-panel-output`](../bottom-panel-output/spec.md). Debug traces MUST call `appendProjectLog` with the **action id** as the message and user text in `context` only (not a duplicate log line). Validation with warnings or errors SHOULD select the **Problems** tab; other actions SHOULD select **Output**.

rules.requirement: graph sidebar opens central overview
rules: Opening the graph entry from the sidebar MUST switch the central workspace to the **Graph Main View**; it MUST NOT append a graph status line to the local output buffer.

rules.requirement: shell orchestrates global command and search navigation
rules:
The shell MUST host a global **Command Palette** (⌘K) and orchestrate navigation from search results:

- Selection of a **Specification** in the palette MUST open that spec in the central workspace.
- Selection of a **Code Symbol** MUST (in v1) log the selection to Output; subsequent versions MAY implement direct source file navigation.
- Selection of a **Document** MUST (in v1) log the selection to Output.

scenarios.requirement: shell provides sidebar tabs inspector and bottom panel regions
scenarios.name: Layout renders titlebar sidebar and inspector regions
then: unified titlebar is visible
AND left shadcn primary sidebar with icon menu is visible
AND central tabs and right inspector hosts exist

scenarios.requirement: shell provides sidebar tabs inspector and bottom panel regions
scenarios.name: macOS desktop titlebar spans full shell width
given: data-platform="darwin"
then: titlebar spans sidebar and main columns
AND sidebar body starts below titlebar height

scenarios.requirement: shell provides sidebar tabs inspector and bottom panel regions
scenarios.name: Non-darwin sidebar is full height with titlebar over main column
given: data-platform is web, win32, or linux
then: sidebar extends to the top of the shell
AND titlebar renders above the main content column only
AND SidebarTrigger is not in the titlebar

scenarios.requirement: shell provides sidebar tabs inspector and bottom panel regions
scenarios.name: Expanded sidebar shows stacked Changes and Workspaces blocks
given: sidebar is expanded
then: Changes compact list is visible
AND Workspaces tree block is visible below Changes
AND no Graph sidebar body panel is present

scenarios.requirement: shell provides sidebar tabs inspector and bottom panel regions
scenarios.name: Collapsed sidebar shows 48px icon rail
when: user toggles sidebar collapsed via SidebarTrigger or ⌘B
then: shadcn sidebar icon width is 48px
AND Changes, Workspaces, and Graph menu icons are visible
AND SpecD Studio branding header is hidden

scenarios.requirement: shell provides sidebar tabs inspector and bottom panel regions
scenarios.name: Bottom panel host is available
then: bottom panel renders with tabs in order Output, Problems, Logs
AND Output is the initially selected tab

scenarios.requirement: shell provides sidebar tabs inspector and bottom panel regions
scenarios.name: Bottom panel tab order is Output Problems Logs
then: left-to-right order is Output, Problems, Logs
AND no other tab precedes Output

scenarios.requirement: shell provides sidebar tabs inspector and bottom panel regions
scenarios.name: Regions survive route navigation
then: chrome persists
AND only tab content swaps

scenarios.requirement: shell provides unified titlebar for global actions
scenarios.name: Titlebar shows search and New Change without branding
then: titlebar contains command-palette search entry
AND New Change action is visible
AND SpecD Studio branding is not in the titlebar

scenarios.requirement: shell provides unified titlebar for global actions
scenarios.name: Desktop titlebar shows inline secondary icons
given: host mode is desktop
when: titlebar renders on a wide viewport
then: Documentation, Notifications, and Appearance render as inline icon buttons
AND no default overflow (⋯) menu hides those actions

scenarios.requirement: shell provides unified titlebar for global actions
scenarios.name: macOS desktop places sidebar toggle after traffic-light slot
given: host mode is desktop and data-platform="darwin"
then: a traffic-light slot precedes SidebarTrigger
AND sidebar header does not sit under native window controls

scenarios.requirement: shell provides unified titlebar for global actions
scenarios.name: Non-darwin embeds sidebar toggle in sidebar chrome
given: data-platform is web, win32, or linux
then: SidebarTrigger appears in the sidebar header
SidebarTrigger is the first rail icon with a divider before other rail icons

scenarios.requirement: shell provides center domain hubs for changes and workspaces
scenarios.name: Changes rail opens changes hub with full names
when: user clicks Changes on the sidebar rail
then: center shows changes hub
AND at least one change name is shown without sidebar truncation

scenarios.requirement: shell provides center domain hubs for changes and workspaces
scenarios.name: Workspaces rail opens workspaces hub
when: user clicks Workspaces on the sidebar rail
then: center shows workspaces hub with full spec paths

scenarios.requirement: shell provides center domain hubs for changes and workspaces
scenarios.name: Graph rail opens graph center view
when: user clicks Graph on the sidebar rail
then: center shows graph main view
AND sidebar does not show a graph body panel

scenarios.requirement: sidebar collapse state is keyboard-toggleable and persisted
scenarios.name: Cmd B toggles sidebar collapsed state
given: sidebar expanded
then: sidebar collapses to activity rail
sidebar expands to previous width

scenarios.requirement: sidebar collapse state is keyboard-toggleable and persisted
scenarios.name: Sidebar state restores from storage
given: user collapsed sidebar in a prior session
then: sidebar restores collapsed state

scenarios.requirement: workspace tree poll skips when Workspaces section is not visible
scenarios.name: Workspaces hub enables workspace tree poll while sidebar collapsed
given: sidebar collapsed and center is workspaces-hub
then: workspace tree hook refetches

scenarios.requirement: workspace tree poll skips when Workspaces section is not visible
scenarios.name: Collapsed sidebar without hub skips workspace tree poll
given: sidebar collapsed to activity rail
then: workspace tree hook does not refetch
AND cached tree data is retained

scenarios.requirement: workspace tree poll skips when Workspaces section is not visible
scenarios.name: Re-expanding sidebar shows cached specs without loading flash
given: sidebar was collapsed with cached workspace tree data
then: Workspaces tree renders immediately from cache
AND no empty loading state is shown while cache is present

scenarios.requirement: workspace tree poll skips when Workspaces section is not visible
scenarios.name: Non-Workspaces hub with collapsed sidebar skips poll
given: sidebar collapsed and center is changes-hub
then: workspace tree hook does not refetch

scenarios.requirement: shell orchestrates global polling while focused
scenarios.name: Global poll refreshes project and sidebars
given: window focused
then: project hooks refetch
AND sidebar lists refetch

scenarios.requirement: shell orchestrates global polling while focused
scenarios.name: Poll does not require open change tab
given: no change tab open
then: workspace tree still updates
AND drafts list still updates

scenarios.requirement: shell orchestrates global polling while focused
scenarios.name: Unfocused window pauses poll
given: window blurred
then: no global poll requests fire

scenarios.requirement: editor area tabs support horizontal scrolling and navigation arrows
scenarios.name: Tabs scroll when overflowing container
given: editor area has more tabs than fit in view
when: user uses scroll wheel or trackpad over tabs
then: tabs scroll horizontally

scenarios.requirement: editor area tabs support horizontal scrolling and navigation arrows
scenarios.name: Navigation arrows appear when overflowing
given: editor area has overflowing tabs
then: left and right navigation arrows are present
AND arrows are disabled when scrolled to extreme ends

scenarios.requirement: tab views display context via breadcrumbs
scenarios.name: Context breadcrumbs render above tabs
given: a change or spec tab is open
then: a breadcrumb header displays the context (e.g. CHANGES / my-change) above the tab bar

scenarios.requirement: global polling pauses when the window is unfocused
scenarios.name: Blur stops global poll interval
then: timers for global poll are cleared or skipped

scenarios.requirement: global polling pauses when the window is unfocused
scenarios.name: Focus resumes polling
then: next poll tick schedules
AND data refreshes

scenarios.requirement: global polling pauses when the window is unfocused
scenarios.name: Hidden change tab does not restart global poll
given: global poll paused by blur
then: global poll remains paused until focus returns

scenarios.requirement: shell never imports @specd/core
scenarios.name: Shell package has no core dependency
then: no import from @specd/core
AND ports used via hooks

scenarios.requirement: shell never imports @specd/core
scenarios.name: Command palette uses hooks only
then: invokes SpecdDataPort path
AND no direct kernel import

scenarios.requirement: shell never imports @specd/core
scenarios.name: Adding core import fails boundary check
then: lint or architectural test fails

scenarios.requirement: shell applies design-system theme at application root
scenarios.name: SpecdApp imports theme before chrome
then: design-system theme module is loaded at root
AND --studio-bg-primary or equivalent tokens apply to the app shell

scenarios.requirement: shell applies design-system theme at application root
scenarios.name: Shell regions use token backgrounds
then: backgrounds use secondary/elevated tokens from design-system
AND not hard-coded marketing card colors

scenarios.requirement: shell applies design-system theme at application root
scenarios.name: Status bar uses design-system chrome
then: status bar uses thin IDE strip styling from tokens
AND border separator uses #30363D token

scenarios.requirement: shell routes archived changes through archived read port
scenarios.name: Archive selection uses getArchivedChange
when: user opens a change from Archive sidebar section
then: shell loads via getArchivedChange
AND inspector save and validate are disabled
AND archived artifact reads use the read-only archived artifact path rather than active getChangeArtifact

scenarios.requirement: shell routes archived changes through archived read port
scenarios.name: Archived change skips getChange polling
given: archived center context
then: shell does not call getChange or getChangeStatus for that name

scenarios.requirement: read-only change tabs load once (no per-change polling)
scenarios.name: Drafted change does not poll status or artifacts
given: drafted change open in the shell
then: shell does not call getDraftStatus repeatedly
AND change artifact list does not refetch on every tick

scenarios.requirement: shell routes drafted and discarded changes through read-only ports
scenarios.name: Draft sidebar selection uses getDraft
given: change dummy-draft is listed under Drafts only
when: user opens it from the sidebar
then: shell passes listSection draft to useChangesRead
AND overview loads without Change 'dummy-draft' not found

scenarios.requirement: shell routes drafted and discarded changes through read-only ports
scenarios.name: Discarded sidebar selection uses getDiscarded
given: change is listed under Discarded only
when: user opens it from the sidebar
then: shell passes listSection discarded
AND does not call getChange for that name

scenarios.requirement: shell routes drafted and discarded changes through read-only ports
scenarios.name: Active change keeps getChange
given: change is in the In progress list
then: shell passes listSection active or null
AND useChangesRead calls getChange

scenarios.requirement: validate requires drift confirmation
scenarios.name: validate requires drift confirmation — primary path
when: Validate and Validate All MUST show [ui:validate-confirm-dialog](../validate-confirm-dialog/spec.md) before
then: behaviour matches the spec requirement
AND no forbidden side effects occur

scenarios.requirement: validate requires drift confirmation
scenarios.name: validate requires drift confirmation — guard path
given: inputs that stress the requirement boundary
then: errors or skips are explicit and documented

scenarios.requirement: shell delegates tab-scoped polling to visible center tabs
scenarios.name: Hidden change tab does not refetch on global tick
given: user is on Overview tab only
then: Artifacts tab hooks do not refetch
AND visible Overview may refetch detail when applicable

scenarios.requirement: opening a spec auto-selects the primary artifact
scenarios.name: Opening a spec preselects spec.md in the inspector
given: workspace spec ui:foo exposes canonical spec.md
when: user opens that spec from the workspace tree
then: the right inspector opens automatically
AND the selected file is spec.md

scenarios.requirement: bottom panel polls only the remote logs channel
scenarios.name: Logs tab does not poll studio output
given: user selects bottom Logs tab
then: no dedicated studio-output poll is invoked
AND readProjectLogs may run

scenarios.requirement: bottom panel polls only the remote logs channel
scenarios.name: Output tab does not poll project logs
given: user selects bottom Output tab
then: readProjectLogs is not invoked
AND Output still renders local buffered entries

scenarios.requirement: bottom panel polls only the remote logs channel
scenarios.name: Switching bottom tabs does not append output
given: local output buffer has N entries
when: user toggles between Output and Logs
then: entry count stays N until an explicit user action appends

scenarios.requirement: metadata and artifact saves log to Output
scenarios.name: successful actions append local output and optional debug trace
when: artifact save, description/policy PATCH, successful scope dialog save, or validation succeeds
then: a local output entry is appended
AND a remote debug trace may be written via appendProjectLog

scenarios.requirement: metadata and artifact saves log to Output
scenarios.name: output buffer discards oldest rows beyond the cap
given: the shell already holds 400 local output entries
when: another action appends a new output entry
then: the oldest entry is discarded
AND total retained entries remain 400

scenarios.requirement: graph sidebar opens central overview
scenarios.name: Opening graph sidebar entry switches center workspace
when: the user opens the graph entry from the sidebar
then: the shell switches to the central Graph Main View
AND local output entry count is unchanged

scenarios.requirement: shell orchestrates global command and search navigation
scenarios.name: Navigating to a spec from palette
when: user selects a Specification from the Command Palette
then: the central workspace switches to the selected spec

scenarios.requirement: shell orchestrates global command and search navigation
scenarios.name: Navigating to a symbol logs to output
when: user selects a Code Symbol from the Command Palette
then: the shell appends a navigation log to the Output panel

scenarios.requirement: shell orchestrates global command and search navigation
scenarios.name: Navigating to a document logs to output
when: user selects a Document from the Command Palette
then: the shell appends a navigation log to the Output panel
